input: Analyze squat kinematics: Left Knee Flexion 138.4 deg, Right Knee Flexion 121.2 deg, Asymmetry 14.1%, Knee Distance 0.18m, Torso Lean 48.2 deg.
output:
DIAGNOSIS: Asymmetrical leg loading with 14.1% knee flexion deficit on the right side. Valgus knee collapse present (0.18m < 0.22m threshold). Excessive forward torso lean (48.2° > 45°).
INJURY RISK: Increased shear force on L4/L5 lumbar spine and patellofemoral joint stress.
RECOMMENDATION: Perform goblet box squats to maintain upright posture and gluteus medius band clamshells (3x15) to correct valgus collapse.

input: Analyze squat kinematics: Left Knee Flexion 122.0 deg, Right Knee Flexion 122.5 deg, Asymmetry 0.4%, Knee Distance 0.28m, Torso Lean 32.1 deg.
output:
DIAGNOSIS: Excellent bilateral symmetry (0.4% asymmetry). Optimal knee alignment (0.28m stance width) with clean upright trunk control (32.1° torso lean).
INJURY RISK: Minimal.
RECOMMENDATION: Progress load progressively while preserving current hip-hinge mechanics.

input: Analyze running gait: Cadence 152 SPM, Left GCT 290ms, Right GCT 240ms, GCT Asymmetry 17.2%, Vertical Oscillation 9.4cm.
output:
DIAGNOSIS: Low step cadence (152 SPM < 170 SPM target) with severe Ground Contact Time asymmetry (17.2%). Excessive vertical oscillation (9.4cm > 7.0cm threshold) indicates wasted upward energy and braking force.
INJURY RISK: High risk of tibial stress fracture and Achilles tendinopathy on the left leg due to prolonged ground load duration.
RECOMMENDATION: Increase running cadence by 7-10% using a metronome app and perform quick-feet ladder drills to shorten ground contact time.
